title: Lamport Signatures
description: Lamport signatures are one-time digital signatures built only from hash functions. They trade large keys and single-use semantics for simple security assumptions, which is why they remain a useful starting point for understanding hash-based and post-quantum signature families.

resources.title: Constructing Digital Signatures from a One Way Function
resources.notes: Leslie Lamport's original paper introducing one-time signatures built from one-way functions.

resources.title: Bitcoin Optech Newsletter #301
resources.notes: Bitcoin-specific context for how Lamport signatures show up in quantum-resistance discussions and proposed script constructions.

resources.title: Programming Bitcoin
resources.notes: General cryptography and signing background for understanding why alternative signature constructions matter in Bitcoin.
